all_citations.claim: The organization maintains a 71.7% program expense ratio, 8.3 months of working capital, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.26 per $1 raised.
The Intercept Media Inc. reported a total revenue of $5,597,043 in the 2023 fiscal year.
The Intercept's mission is to use journalism as an instrument of civic activism to investigate corruption and injustice.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a moderate score reflecting solid financial management and operational efficiency, balanced against the indirect nature of advocacy-based impact.
areas_for_improvement: Limited evidence of direct service impact, as the organization focuses on broad capacity-building and advocacy
Lower alignment for traditional Muslim donor priorities compared to direct-aid humanitarian causes

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is considered moderate as the cause focuses on high-level advocacy and institutional accountability rather than traditional charitable services [3]. While it offers a platform for sadaqah-eligible giving, its mission may have lower resonance for donors seeking high-urgency humanitarian interventions [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates moderate impact through its capacity-building model, focusing on investigative journalism and podcasts to drive systemic change [1][2]. While financial health is stable with a 71.7% program expense ratio, the direct effectiveness of journalism on specific beneficiary outcomes is harder to quantify than direct-aid programs [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Intercept Media Inc. serves as a critical watchdog, utilizing investigative journalism to expose corruption and institutional injustice.
strengths: Maintains a program expense ratio of 71.7%, ensuring the majority of funds support journalism and filmmaking [1]
Strong liquidity with 8.3 months of working capital available to sustain operations [1]
Efficient fundraising operations, spending only $0.26 to raise every $1 [1]
baseline_narrative.summary: The Intercept Media Inc. is a non-profit news organization dedicated to holding powerful individuals and institutions accountable through adversarial journalism and documentary filmmaking [1][3]. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $5,597,043 to support its mission of civic activism through reporting [2].

areas_for_improvement.context: The current <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 71.7%</cite> is below the 70% threshold typically favored by donors for high-efficiency ratings.
With <cite id="14">officer compensation totaling $1,973,819</cite>, executive pay exceeds 2% of revenue, which may warrant closer board scrutiny for an organization of this size.

risk_factors: <cite id="14">High executive compensation relative to revenue</cite>

alignment.explanation: As a <cite id="18">Muslim-focused media organization</cite> in the advocacy space, it offers a niche for donors interested in systemic change.

credibility.explanation: Donors can verify the organization's standing through its <cite id="9">Platinum Seal from Candid</cite> and its <cite id="12">independent board oversight</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity could <cite id="16">seek external evaluation</cite> of its journalistic impact and track outcomes over a longer horizon.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who believe in the power of adversarial journalism to drive systemic reform and want to support a high-transparency watchdog organization.
donor_motivations: Value the role of the press in holding government and corporate power accountable
Interested in supporting investigative reporting on international human rights
Prefer funding systemic advocacy over direct humanitarian relief
Seek organizations with the highest level of Candid transparency
giving_considerations: Donations are sadaqah-eligible only and do not fulfill zakat obligations
Impact is measured through policy influence and public awareness rather than individual lives saved
A significant portion of the budget is dedicated to administrative and legal safeguards

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization tracks <cite id="20">outcome metrics</cite> but lacks third-party evaluations of its causal impact.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The Intercept uses investigative reporting to inform the public and spark civic action, leading to institutional accountability.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in journalism helps donors understand how specific stories lead to legislative or social changes.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The organization is currently <cite id="21">spending significantly more than its revenue</cite>, indicating a high need for sustainable donor support.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="11">Field of Vision documentary unit</cite> and international reach.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Recipient of Intercept grants for collaborative reporting
similar_organizations.name: Drop Site News Inc

strengths.detail: The organization is highly efficient in its development efforts, requiring only <cite id="6">$0.08 in expenses to generate $1 in revenue</cite>.
The charity maintains <cite id="8">8.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a stable buffer for its investigative operations.
The Intercept has earned a <cite id="9">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available.

strengths_deep_dive: The Intercept's operational strength lies in its ability to leverage a <cite id="10">platinum-tier transparency profile</cite> to secure significant public support, as evidenced by nearly <cite id="4">$18.75 million in annual contributions</cite>. This funding supports a diverse array of media programs including <cite id="11">podcasts, documentary filmmaking through Field of Vision, and whistleblower support</cite>. Furthermore, the organization demonstrates a commitment to accountability through its <cite id="12">independent board of 4 members</cite> and its role as a grantmaker, recently providing <cite id="13">$280,000 in domestic grants</cite> to support the broader journalism ecosystem.
rich_narrative.summary: The Intercept Media Inc. operates as a non-profit news organization dedicated to <cite id="1">investigating powerful individuals and institutions</cite> to expose corruption and institutional injustice. Founded in <cite id="2">2014</cite>, the organization views journalism as an instrument of civic action, focusing on adversarial reporting and documentary filmmaking. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $18,961,623</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="4">$18,747,165 in contributions</cite>. Despite its significant investigative output, the charity maintains a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 71.7%</cite>, which is lower than many direct-service humanitarian peers but reflects the high costs of specialized investigative labor and legal protections. The organization demonstrates high fundraising efficiency, spending only <cite id="6">$0.08 to raise every $1</cite>. Beyond its domestic reporting, The Intercept maintains a global footprint with coverage extending to <cite id="7">Syria, Iran, Mexico, and Ecuador</cite>, providing a platform for transparency and whistleblower support in regions where press freedom is often curtailed.
